about_subtitle: A home for timeless words: curated, searchable, and crafted for daily inspiration.
about_profile_alt: Photo of Jérémie, the creator of Verbatims
about_intro_1: I'm Jérémie, a software engineer and lifelong quote collector.
about_intro_2: I built Verbatims to solve a personal problem: how to keep track of the many quotes I love, along with their sources and context, in a way that makes them easy to find and share.
about_intro_3: I have a background in building web applications and a passion for thoughtful design, so this project is a blend of my interests in technology, literature, and curation.
about_drawing_caption: A drawing I did titled "More than Words" inspired by the Anime "Jujutsu Kaisen". See more of my art on
about_purpose_1: Verbatims is built to share quotes with people in an easy and fun way. The core idea is simple: make discovery, attribution, and sharing delightful. Whether you're hunting for a line to brighten someone's day, building a collection for a talk, or researching a citation, Verbatims aims to make the journey smooth and reliable.
about_history_1: We love note-taking and collecting quotes, but keeping them scattered in docs and screenshots made them hard to find, verify, and share. Verbatims began as a simple tool to bring quotes, sources, and context together in one place.
about_history_2: The vision: a thoughtful, accurate, and inviting library where you can browse by topic or author, build personal collections, and get gentle nudges to revisit ideas that matter to you.
about_history_3: This project has gone through multiple re-dos. It started as a Windows Phone app called Citamour, evolved through several prototypes and rewrites, and eventually became the web-focused project you see today: Verbatims. Each rebuild taught us about discoverability, moderation, and the importance of good metadata.
about_features_1: Browse and search by topic, author, keyword, or reference. Relevant results surface fast, with smart ranking.
about_features_2: Save favorites and build collections: keep what resonates close and organized.
about_features_3: Get a daily quote notification tailored to your interests and recent activity.
about_features_4: Share beautifully formatted cards to social media, or copy clean text with attribution.
about_features_5: Contribute your own quotes with sources. Submissions go through moderation for accuracy and clarity.
about_curation_1: Quotes are sourced from primary texts when possible, reputable anthologies, scholarly databases, and trusted archives. Each quote includes attribution and, where available, reference details.
about_curation_2: We verify wording and authorship before publishing. When a quote is widely misattributed, we either correct it with a note or decline it. We also welcome community reports with citations. Corrections help everyone.
about_curation_3: Editorial guidelines prioritize clarity, context, and respectful representation. Paraphrases are labeled; ellipses and edits are clearly indicated.
about_community_1: Submit quotes with sources directly from quote pages or your dashboard.
about_community_2: Report issues (typos, attribution, duplicates) using the "Report" action on any quote.
about_community_3: Suggest features and improvements from the settings "Feedback" link.
about_community_4: Participate respectfully: celebrate ideas, not individuals. Harassment, hate, or plagiarism isn't allowed.
about_accessibility_1: We design for readability and keyboard navigation, with high-contrast themes and scalable type. The interface supports screen readers with semantic landmarks and descriptive labels.
about_accessibility_2: Language is for everyone: we're expanding multilingual support and prioritizing diverse voices across cultures, eras, and perspectives. You'll find classic thinkers alongside contemporary authors, poets, scientists, and activists.
about_privacy_1: We keep it simple. We store the essentials to make the app work for you: your account info, favorites, collections, language preferences, and notification settings. Usage data may be aggregated to improve search and recommendations.
about_privacy_2: We don't sell personal data. For full details, see our
about_roadmap_1: Richer author pages with timelines, references, and related works.
about_roadmap_2: Advanced filters: sources, eras, movements, and reading level.
about_roadmap_3: Scheduled shares and export options for newsletters and slides.
about_roadmap_4: Better multilingual search with transliteration support.
about_roadmap_5: Opt-in AI suggestions trained on curated, cited sources.
about_tech_intro: Verbatims is built with modern web tooling. The main pieces include:
about_tech_1: Nuxt 3 (Vue 3) for the application framework and server rendering.
about_tech_3: UnoCSS + UNA UI for utility-first styling and UI primitives.
about_tech_4: Nuxt Image for image handling and optimization.
about_tech_5: Server-side features powered by Nitro; Cloudflare / Wrangler used in CI/deploy workflows.
about_suggestions_intro: A few ideas I'd like to explore next:
about_suggestions_1: Richer export options (CSV, JSON, and print-ready quote cards).
about_suggestions_2: Improved author pages with timelines and source documents.
about_suggestions_3: Multilingual UI and search improvements for non-Latin scripts.
about_suggestions_4: Opt-in personalization and a gentle recommendation layer.
about_contact_1: Questions, ideas, or found a hiccup? We'd love to hear from you. Open a GitHub issue or use the feedback form in the header
about_contact_tagline: Built with care, curiosity, and plenty of coffee.
